channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch on the fight to split off models from agents
item.description: "The reality is, when you're optimizing for production, you start looking at a price/performance," Guillermo Rauch tells TechCrunch.

item.title: Claude : l’application web d’Anthropic subit une panne, mais son API et Claude Code fonctionnent
item.description: Claude, l'assistant IA d'Anthropic, connaît des ratés côté application web, alors que l'API et Claude Code, l'outil pour développeurs, semblent tenir le coup.

item.title: Google now uses your uploaded search media to train AI
item.description: A few simple adjustments to your settings will opt you out of Google's latest AI-training method.

item.title: You can now customize Siri’s pace and expressivity in the latest iOS 27 beta
item.description: The update is part of Apple's broader effort to make Siri feel more natural and personal, as it rebuilds the assistant around generative AI.

item.title: ‘I am mortified’: An iPhone user sent ‘sensitive pictures’ to a stranger’s phone after making this simple iMessage mistake — here’s how to avoid the same fate
item.description: An iPhone user accidentally sent ‘sensitive’ texts to the wrong person after their partner made one key mistake.

item.title: Apple déploie la bêta 3 d’iOS 27 : le point sur le nouveau Siri AI et les iPhone compatibles
item.description: Apple vient de publier la troisième bêta développeur d’iOS 27. Une étape de plus vers Siri AI, l’assistant repensé qui reste bloqué en Europe au lancement.

item.title: The lightest Linux laptop ever just launched, and it challenges the MacBook Air for battery life
item.description: The refreshed Lemur Pro is a Linux-based ultraportable that offers plenty of power and battery life under the hood.

item.title: America’s greatest idea is still under threat
item.description: The United States of America recently turned 250 years old. What a spectacle! The fireworks were amazing, and millions of proud people celebrated across the nation - even around the world. France lit up the Eiffel Tower; Japan had fireworks. French fighter jets flew above New York City with trails of red, white, and blue […]

item.title: Every major tech layoff in 2026 that has name-checked AI
item.description: A running look — in reverse chronological order — at the bigger tech companies that have announced significant layoffs this year with AI as a stated factor.

item.title: Why are people ditching wireless earbuds, and are wired headphones really making a comeback?
Klarna wants to become a bank. Here’s what that could mean for millions of customers
item.description: The fintech giant has applied for a U.S. banking charter, paving the way for checking accounts, loans, and other financial services under its own name. The buy now, pay later fintech company Klarna wants to become a real bank.

item.title: This Anker Solix deal is the ultimate summer power setup — save $530 on the record-breaking C1000X Gen 2 I’d buy for camping
item.description: 1024Wh, 2000W output, and a Guinness World Record-breaking 49-minute recharge.

item.title: This $810 mini PC beats heavy engineering laptops — with 32GB upgradeable RAM and OCuLink power, it’s the ultimate dorm buy
item.description: Don't want to cart a 6lb laptop across campus? This mini PC is dorm room-ready for engineering projects.

item.title: Amazon competitor Bookshop.org says Kobo eReader support will happen this year after all
item.description: Bookship.org seemed to delay this anticipated partnership again, but tells TechCrunch that it has settled business terms and is working on integration.

item.title: FlexiSpot’s C7 is one of our top-rated office chairs — and this deal is a steal for all-day comfort
item.description: 'An affordable, luxury ergonomic office chair for all-day comfort,' was our verdict after tests.

item.title: Éclipse du Soleil, étoiles filantes, Saturne… : voici le programme parfait pour lever les yeux en famille cet été
item.description: Cet été, le ciel réserve plusieurs rendez-vous faciles à observer, à condition de savoir quoi chercher et quand regarder. Voici le guide simple et concret pour repérer l’éclipse du 12 août, reconnaître une planète, profiter des étoiles filantes et commencer à observer avec ses propres yeux, des...
